blocks.content: The first hour was easy. It went the way all good nights do when everyone has been apart long enough to have things to say.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I study them and also make them, yes.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: His film screened in a senior class. As a freshman.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: How do you know about that.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You were going to downplay it. Someone had to tell people correctly.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: This is what they're like now. All the time. It's great.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I know. That's why I'm saying it to your face.

Kit reached over and stole her drink. She took it back without looking at him. The group noticed. Nobody said anything. That was the thing about this group — they knew when to push and when to let things be what they were.
